purpose: Give another LLM or designer enough structure to create a wide red/green dot matrix showing solo-model brittleness and HoloVerify coverage without confusing the public denominator.
primary_message: Solo AI is often right, but scattered failures remain across high-stakes action boundaries. HoloVerify is tested as a governed layer that should cover those failures without hiding misses.

claim_boundary.solo_matrix_status: internal stress evidence, not public FPR/FNR denominator
claim_boundary.holo_overlay_status: internal rescue or hardening evidence unless separately admitted
forbidden_claims: The stress matrix is the public error-rate denominator
The old 614 result is combined with blind-120
Internal V5/V6 repair lanes are public benchmark proof

visual_layout.wide_view_goal: Show a large field of mostly green dots with scattered red/yellow failures across domains.
visual_layout.zoom_view_goal: When a user clicks or zooms into one square, show the six solo dots and the HoloVerify coverage result clearly.
visual_layout.columns: packet pairs or grouped test cases
visual_layout.cell_contains: six solo opportunity dots plus optional HoloVerify overlay
visual_layout.recommended_orientation: wide horizontal matrix, domains stacked vertically, pairs running left to right
visual_layout.mobile_behavior: show domain filters first, then horizontally scrollable rows, then tap-to-expand cell detail
visual_layout.desktop_behavior: show the wide matrix, hover tooltip, click to open zoom panel

green.label: Correct
green.meaning: Solo model gave the correct and admissible action-boundary verdict.

red.label: Wrong call
red.meaning: Solo model made the wrong action-boundary verdict.

yellow.label: Format failure
yellow.meaning: Solo model failed parse or admissibility rules.

gray.label: Quarantined
gray.meaning: Packet or key defect. Do not count.

holo_overlay.green_ring: HoloVerify covered a solo failure in an internal rescue or hardening lane.
holo_overlay.red_ring: HoloVerify also failed or the failure is still unresolved.

holo_result.score: covered in internal Holo rescue lane

tooltip.headline: Solo model allowed an action that should have escalated.
tooltip.plain_language_summary: The packet looked close, but the evidence did not authorize the requested action.
tooltip.why_it_is_hard: The allow and escalate siblings differ by a narrow authority or scope detail.
tooltip.solo_failure_summary: One of six solo opportunities failed.
tooltip.holo_summary: HoloVerify covered this failure in an internal hardening lane.
tooltip.counting_status: internal stress evidence, not public denominator

generation_instructions_for_llm: Create a wide visual matrix with domains as rows and sibling pairs as columns.
Each cell must contain exactly six dots arranged as two rows of three.
Use short plain-English labels. Avoid jargon in the visible UI.
Include a toggle with three states: Solo view, Holo overlay, Counting status.
In Solo view, show only the six solo dots.
In Holo overlay, preserve the solo dots and add rings or overlays to show whether Holo covered the failure.
In Counting status, shade cells by public counted, internal only, quarantined, or historical.
Make the first view wide enough to show the pattern: mostly green, scattered red/yellow.
Make zoom view explain one square in plain English.
Never label the stress matrix as the public FPR/FNR denominator.

copy_blocks.short_intro: Solo AI is often right. The problem is the scattered misses. In high-stakes work, scattered red dots are enough to require a governed action-boundary layer.
copy_blocks.matrix_warning: This matrix is a failure atlas, not one blended benchmark score. The public denominator is blind-120 only.
copy_blocks.zoom_caption: Each square is one paired test. The six dots are three solo models across the Allow and Escalate sibling cases.
copy_blocks.holo_overlay_caption: The Holo overlay shows whether HoloVerify covered the solo failure, failed too, or excluded the packet.
